body_excerpt: {"credits": [], "cve_id": "CVE-2026-48006", "cvss": {"score": 7.5, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:N/I:N/A:H"}, "cvss_severities": {"cvss_v3": {"score": 7.5, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:N/I:N/A:H"}, "cvss_v4": {"score": 8.7, "vector_string": "CVSS:4.0/AV:N/AC:L/AT:N/PR:N/UI:N/VC:N/VI:N/VA:H/SC:N/SI:N/SA:N"}}, "cwes": [{"cwe_id": "CWE-401", "name": "Missing Release of Memory after Effective Lifetime"}, {"cwe_id": "CWE-772", "name": "Missing Release of Resource after Effective Lifetime"}], "description": "### Impact\nThe RedisArrayAggregator handler permanently leaks pooled direct-memory buffers when a Redis pipeline connection closes before a RESP array aggregate completes. The handler retains child messages in per-handler state (`depths` field) but defines no `channelInactive`, `handlerRemoved`, or `exceptionCaught` method to release them when the pipeline tears down. Because the leaked buffers are slices of `PooledByteBufAllocator` chunks, they prevent those chunks from being returned to the JVM-wide direct-memory pool. Repeated connection churn by any network peer monotonically drains this shared pool, eventually causing allocation failures on all Netty channels in the process.", "epss": {"percentage": 0.005, "percentile": 0.39502}, "ghsa_id": "GHSA-6jv9-x5w9-2ccm", "github_reviewed_at": "2026-06-11T13:26:06Z", "html_url": "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-6jv9-x5w9-2ccm", "identifiers": [{"type": "GHSA", "value": "GHSA-6jv9-x5w9-2ccm"}, {"type": "CVE", "value": "CVE-2026-48006"}], "nvd_published_at": "2026-06-12T16:16:30Z", "published_at": "2026-06-11T13:26:06Z", "references": ["https://github.com/netty/netty/security/advisories/GHSA-6jv9-x5w9-2ccm", "https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2026-48006", "https://github.com/netty/netty/releases/tag/netty-4.1.135.Final", "https://github.com/netty/netty/releases/tag/netty-4.2.15.Final", "https://access.redhat.com/security/cve/CVE-2026-48006", "https://bugzilla.redhat.com/show_bug.cgi?id=2488433", "https://security.access.redhat.com/data/csaf/v2/vex/2026/cve-2026-48006.json", "https://access.redhat.com/errata/RHSA-2026:37390", "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-6jv9-x5w9-2ccm"], "repository_advisory_url": "https://api.github.com/repos/netty/netty/security-advisories/GHSA-6jv9-x5w9-2ccm", "severity": "high", "source_code_location": "https://github.com/netty/netty", "summary": "Netty's Lack of Lifecycle Cleanup Leads to Pooled ByteBuf Leak in RedisArrayAggregator", "type": "reviewed", "updated_at": "2026-07-10T12:31:47Z", "url": "https://api.github.com/advisories/GHSA-6jv9-x5w9-2ccm", "vulnerabilities": [{"first_patched_version": "4.2.15.Final", "package": {"ecosystem": "maven", "name": "io.netty:netty-codec-redis"}, "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": ">= 4.2.0.Final, <= 4.2.14.Final"}, {"first_patched_version": "4.1.135.Final", "package": {"ecosystem": "maven", "name": "io.netty:netty-codec-redis"}, "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": "<= 4.1.134.Final"}], "withdrawn_at": null}

title: Netty's Lack of Lifecycle Cleanup Leads to Pooled ByteBuf Leak in RedisArrayAggregator
